title: Schema Field Order — Autoregressive Steering
one_liner: Place dependent fields AFTER their dependencies in structured-output schemas; the model generates left-to-right and later fields can use earlier ones.

title: Weak-Model Preselection
one_liner: Run a cheap model first to filter/classify/rank inputs (returning only refs/IDs), then pass the filtered set to the strong model for actual reasoning.

title: File-Reference Passing Between Pipeline Steps
one_liner: Pass references (paths/IDs/URLs), not content, between agent stages — the next stage loads on demand. Cuts context cost and improves recoverability.

title: Semantic Field Naming
one_liner: Field names are part of the prompt — specific English names trigger pre-trained priors; renaming final_choice→answer swung accuracy 4.5%→95%.

title: Field Descriptions as Inline Mini-Prompts
one_liner: Each Field(description=) is a tiny per-field prompt; precise descriptions covering format/units/edge-cases lift extraction accuracy 60%+.

title: Tool Description as Prompt
one_liner: Tool descriptions ARE the prompt the model uses to pick tools and fill arguments — structure them as use-when / NOT-use-when / I/O contract / side-effects.

title: Prompt-Cache Prefix Order
one_liner: Order prompts stable→volatile (system→tools→retrieval→history→user); place cache_control after the longest stable prefix; cuts ~90% on cached portion.

title: Subagent as Context Firewall
one_liner: Use subagents to keep heavy work in isolated contexts; they return slim summary+refs only — parent's context grows ~500 tokens regardless of subagent's read volume.

title: Stream-JSON Orchestration
one_liner: Spawn agent CLIs (Claude Code/Codex/opencode) with --output-format stream-json so the orchestrator can react mid-run — budget caps, hand-offs, safety vetoes.

title: Plan-Execute vs ReAct — Picking the Right Loop
one_liner: Plan-Execute when steps are predictable (auditable, parallel); ReAct when next step depends on observation; Hybrid (plan top, ReAct per-step) for long horizons.

title: Trajectory Evaluation with OTel GenAI Spans
one_liner: Score agent runs on 3 axes (outcome/trajectory/resource) using OTel GenAI semantic conventions; single-axis evals miss cost and path regressions.

title: MCP — Resource vs Tool vs Prompt: Three-Question Test
one_liner: Classify each MCP capability with three questions: needs to act? → Tool; static-per-URI content? → Resource; user-invoked template? → Prompt.

title: Embedded Scratchpad Field
one_liner: Add reasoning/plan_steps/evidence field BEFORE the answer in strict JSON schemas; gives free CoT inside structured output (+60% on GSM8k).

title: Confidence-Thresholded Cascade
one_liner: Cheap model answers with confidence; if ≥ threshold accept, else escalate to strong model — FrugalGPT pattern saves up to 98% cost matching best-LLM quality.

title: Bundle vs Split Tools
one_liner: Default split (many small tools); bundle into mode-arg tools only when count > 25 AND modes share audience+args; tool-selection accuracy collapses past ~25 flat tools.

title: Auto-Evict Tool Results at Token Threshold
one_liner: Runtime middleware writes any tool result over N tokens (default 20k) to disk and substitutes a path+preview manifest before the LLM ever sees the body.

title: Claude Code Headless As Default Agent Runtime
one_liner: Default to `claude -p` subprocess for coding/devops/research agents; switch to Agent SDK only on a concrete trigger (non-coding domain, custom non-MCP tools, inner-loop control, or SaaS embedding).

title: Headless CLI — Four Guards Against Mystery Hangs
one_liner: Every non-interactive agent CLI run MUST set print flag + tool allowlist + max-turns + closed stdin; missing any one maps to a known production hang or runaway-loop.

title: Max-Turns as Circuit Breaker, Not Error
one_liner: Cap every agent run with explicit max_turns; catch MaxTurnsExceeded and summarize partial trace with cheap model — only deterministic stop for tool-loops that fail by silent budget burn.

title: PostToolUse Hook as Self-Correction Loop
one_liner: Wire PostToolUse hooks (Write|Edit) to run lint/typecheck after every mutation; non-zero stderr feeds back as tool error and the agent self-corrects on the next turn.

title: Generator-Critic Loop with Hard Cap and Delta Exit
one_liner: Bound reflection at 3 iterations; exit on critic veto, score plateau, or cap; cheap critic for rubric tasks, same-tier critic for correctness.

title: Map-Reduce Fan-Out with Bounded Concurrency and Idempotent Branches
one_liner: Use Send-style fan-out for runtime-variable N with annotated reducer, cap concurrency at 20, require idempotent branches because super-steps fail atomically.

title: Discriminated-Union Structured Output
one_liner: Use Pydantic discriminated unions (Field(discriminator=kind)) for polymorphic output; the literal kind generated first locks the rest of the branch via grammar.

title: Strict-Mode Required-Everything + No Additional Properties
one_liner: In OpenAI/Azure strict mode every property must be required and additionalProperties:false; encode optionality as nullable T|None, never by omitting from required.

title: Preference-Trained Router (RouteLLM Pattern)
one_liner: Train a tiny router on preference data to pick weak vs strong model BEFORE inference; one round-trip, 95% GPT-4 quality at 26% strong-model traffic.

title: Role-Specialized Models per Agent Step
one_liner: Pin strong model to plan/review, mid model to execute, small model to classify/extract — split by cognitive role, not by step number.

title: Rerank Before Reasoning (Two-Stage Retrieval)
one_liner: Retrieve 50-200 candidates with embeddings, rerank with a cross-encoder to top 5-10, THEN feed the reasoner; +10-20 pp precision@10.

title: Cost-Aware Gateway with Fallback Chain (OpenRouter Pattern)
one_liner: Route every production call through a gateway with cross-provider fallback chain; bill only success — keeps tail availability above any single vendor.

title: Progressive Disclosure Skills — 3-Level Pyramid
one_liner: Author Skills as a 3-level pyramid (frontmatter always loaded, SKILL.md on activation, scripts/references on Read) so base context stays small as skill count grows.

title: Filesystem as Working Memory — Offload Before Summarize
one_liner: Offload tool results >2000 tokens to a write_file/read_file/ls/grep FS and pass paths; only summarize conversation past 85% utilization when nothing left to offload.

title: previous_response_id — Reasoning-Item Reuse on the Responses API
one_liner: Chain reasoning-model turns via previous_response_id (or encrypted_content under ZDR) to inherit reasoning items; rebuilding message arrays drops them and regresses agentic benchmarks 20-40%.

title: LLM-as-Judge — Rubric, Evidence-First, Bias-Mitigated
one_liner: Judge open-ended outputs only with named rubric, JSON schema, evidence quoted before scoring, swap-order pairs, and cross-family judges to neutralise the four canonical biases.

title: Record / Replay — Deterministic Agent Debugging
one_liner: Architect every agent with record (capture every LLM/tool/RNG boundary) and replay (serve them back, zero network) modes so production failures reproduce deterministically and one-variable mutation verifies fixes.

title: Chaos-Eval — Fault Injection on Agent Tools
one_liner: Inject the five canonical tool faults (timeout, error, rate-limit, mutate, disconnect) at random and deterministic points; grade on recovered / retried / escalated / wrong-confident — never aggregate success rate alone.

title: Manifest-Then-Fetch — Two-Phase Tool Result
one_liner: Tools return a small manifest envelope; full body lives externally and is loaded only via explicit get_full_result(execution_id) — preview-only by default.

title: Compaction Templates That Preserve References
one_liner: Compaction prompt enforces a fixed YAML schema (paths, decisions, errors, refs, next_action) — preserves pointers verbatim, drops reasoning and tool bodies.

title: Handoff Payload — ID + Minimal Metadata
one_liner: Multi-agent handoff carries {task_id, target_agent, decision_metadata} only; receiver pulls task state from a shared store keyed by task_id.
